title: Fruit Jar Glow
central_metaphor: Captured fireflies as a "borrowed sunlight" representing the temporary, magical innocence of childhood summers, contrasted with the permanent, artificial light of adulthood.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
A mason jar of lightning bugs and holes
Punched in the lid with one of mama’s poles
We’d shake our little lanterns in the air
And pretend we’d captured every star up there
[Pre-Chorus]
The dusk would settle soft as talcum powder
The grown-up voices getting a bit louder
[Chorus]
A living nightlight in a fruit jar glow
A secret that the summer used to know
We’d set them free before we went inside
With all that borrowed sunlight in our stride
[Verse 2]
The fireflies still flicker in that field
But now the high grass makes me slow to yield
I watch them from the driver’s side alone
A silent, blinking signal on my phone
[Pre-Chorus]
The dark comes quicker than it did before
And I can’t find that magic anymore
[Chorus]
A living nightlight in a fruit jar glow
A secret that the summer used to know
We’d set them free before we went inside
With all that borrowed sunlight in our stride
[Bridge]
I bought a string of electric, plastic ones
For my kid’s room, to mimic all those suns
He asked me why they didn’t fly away
I didn’t have the honest words to say
[Final Chorus]
A living nightlight in a fruit jar glow
A secret that the summer used to know
We’d set them free before we went inside
**With all that borrowed darkness we could hide**
